heading: GAO study of financial regulations
label: 5496b GAO study of financial regulations
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this Act, referred to in subsec. (a), probably means the date of enactment of , which enacted this section and was approved .Pub. L. 112–10Apr. 15, 2011

children.text: the impact of regulation on the financial marketplace, including the effects on the safety and soundness of regulated entities, cost and availability of credit, savings realized by consumers, reductions in consumer paperwork burden, changes in personal and small business bankruptcy filings, and costs of compliance with rules, including whether relevant Federal agencies are applying sound cost-benefit analysis in promulgating rules;

efforts to avoid duplicative or conflicting rulemakings, including an evaluation of the consultative process under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of , information requests, and examinations; andsection 5512(b)(2) of this title
other matters related to the operations of financial services regulations deemed by the Comptroller General to be appropriate.

children.heading: Report
children.label: b Report
children.text: Not later than the end of the 30-day period following the completion of a study conducted pursuant to subsection (a), the Comptroller General shall issue a report to the Congress containing a detailed description of all findings and conclusions made by the Comptroller General in carrying out such study, together with such recommendations for legislative or administrative action as the Comptroller General may determine to be appropriate.
